name: Cosmological lithium-7 problem
requirement: Any candidate framework should account for the persistent ~3-4 sigma discrepancy between the BBN-predicted primordial ^7Li abundance and the value inferred from spectroscopy of metal-poor halo stars. Standard BBN with the Planck-determined baryon density predicts ^7Li/H ~ (4.7 +/- 0.7) x 10^-10, while the Spite-plateau measurement gives ^7Li/H ~ (1.6 +/- 0.3) x 10^-10. The ratio is about a factor of 3.

regime.description: BBN epoch (T ~ 30-100 keV) for production; metal-poor halo stars (Pop II, [Fe/H] < -1.5) for observed value.

current_bound.value: BBN prediction: ^7Li/H = (4.7 +/- 0.7) x 10^-10; Observed (Spite plateau): (1.6 +/- 0.3) x 10^-10
current_bound.experiment: Spectroscopy of metal-poor halo stars (Sbordone et al. 2010 and updates)
current_bound.reference: Fields, Olive, Yeh, Young, 'Big-Bang Nucleosynthesis after Planck', JCAP 03, 010 (2020), arXiv:1912.01132; Sbordone et al., A&A 522, A26 (2010)

what_it_excludes: Possible explanations: (a) stellar atmospheric depletion of lithium in halo stars (atomic-diffusion + turbulent mixing models partially work but don't fully account for the dispersion); (b) systematic in nuclear cross sections, especially the ^7Be(d,p) reaction (LUNA experiment continuing measurements); (c) BSM physics during BBN such as charged-particle catalyzed BBN, decaying particles, or modified expansion. The first two are widely viewed as the most likely resolutions.

parameterization.name: Standard BBN reaction network (parameterized by eta, N_eff, neutron lifetime, and ~12 nuclear cross sections); plus stellar-atmosphere depletion factor f_dep; plus optional BSM contributions parameterized by decay lifetime tau and abundance Y of injected particles.
parameterization.reference: Cyburt, Fields, Olive, Yeh, RMP 88, 015004 (2016); Iocco et al., Phys. Rep. 472, 1 (2009).
parameterization.summary: Baseline BBN prediction is a function of eta_b alone given fixed nuclear cross sections; deviations require BSM input.

procedural.predicted_observable: Primordial ^7Li/H ratio, modified by post-BBN processing in stars.
procedural.bound_in_parameterization: Tension at the factor-of-3 level. Resolution requires either non-standard stellar diffusion or a ~10-30% revision of the ^7Be production / destruction cross-section network or BSM intervention.
framework_inputs: If the framework predicts post-BBN energy injection (decaying or annihilating particles, primordial black hole evaporation), compute the change in light-element abundances using AlterBBN or PRIMAT.
If the framework affects nuclear cross sections (varying constants, modified electromagnetic / strong couplings during BBN), compute their effect on the ^7Li reaction network.

primary_references.citation: Fields, Olive, Yeh, Young, 'Big-Bang Nucleosynthesis after Planck', JCAP 03, 010 (2020)
Sbordone et al., 'The metal-poor end of the Spite plateau. I. Stellar parameters, metallicities, and lithium abundances', A&A 522, A26 (2010)
Cyburt, Fields, Olive, Yeh, 'Big bang nucleosynthesis: Present status', RMP 88, 015004 (2016)
